description_long: This object is a composite tree structure featuring three distinct tree forms fused together. On one side, there is a conical evergreen tree with dense, needle-like foliage, reminiscent of a spruce or pine. Adjacent to it is a leafless deciduous tree with a thick trunk and spreading, bare branches, suggesting a winter or dormant state. The third section displays a broadleaf tree with a twisting trunk and sparse, light green foliage, evoking a spring or early summer appearance. The trunks of all three trees are intertwined at the base, creating a unique, sculptural form. The overall appearance is that of three different tree species merged into a single, continuous trunk, with each section representing a different season or type of tree. This object would stand out in an urban park or as a decorative element in a landscaped area.
description: A sculptural tree with three fused sections: a dense evergreen, a bare deciduous, and a sparse broadleaf, all sharing a twisted trunk. Each section represents a different tree type and season.
description_view_0: Front view shows the twisted trunk and the leafy broadleaf section on the left, with the conical evergreen at the top.
description_view_1: Side view reveals all three tree types: broadleaf, bare deciduous, and evergreen, each clearly separated.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the evergreen section, with the broadleaf and bare branches partially visible behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the evergreen on the left, bare deciduous in the center, and broadleaf on the right.
